children.code:
def CheckStock(items: list) -> Tuple[bool, list]:
    product_details_list = []
    stock_ok_list = []
    for item in items:
        product_id = item['product_id']
        quantity = item['quantity']
        details, ok = GetProductDetails(product_id, quantity)
        product_details_list.append(details)
        stock_ok_list.append(ok)
    stock_ok, product_details = AggregateStockResults(items, product_details_list, stock_ok_list)
    return stock_ok, product_details

decomposition_rationale: The parent Order_prd handles multiple commands. Each command is decomposed into a separate child function that directly implements the corresponding functional requirement. The parent function (manage_order) acts as a coordinator: it parses the command and dispatches to the appropriate child, passing the necessary inputs from order_data. Each child performs its specific operation using the available data interfaces. This decomposition ensures each child has a single responsibility, minimal overlap, and covers all parent requirements. The children collectively cover all global variable operations: CreateOrder uses users (read), products (read_write), orders (write); PayOrder uses orders (read_write) and users (read_write); ShipOrder and CompleteOrder use orders (read_write); CancelOrder uses orders (read_write), products (read_write), users (read_write); ListOrders and GetUserOrders use orders (read); ListProducts uses products (read). The union of children's global_vars covers all parent's global_vars.

subprd.purpose: Order Management System
subprd.description:
INPUT FORMAT:
  Format: json
  Description: Input is a JSON object with command and order_data fields, passed to manage_order function.
  Schema:
    command: string (one of: create_order, pay_order, ship_order, complete_order, cancel_order, list_orders, get_user_orders, list_products)
    order_data: object containing relevant fields: user_id (int), order_id (int), items (list of {product_id: int, quantity: int}), status_filter (string), user_filter (int), low_stock (bool)
  Examples:
    {"command": "create_order", "order_data": {"user_id": 1, "items": [{"product_id": 2, "quantity": 2}]}}

OUTPUT FORMAT:
  Format: json
  Description: Output is a JSON object with success, message, and data fields.
  Schema:
    success: boolean
    message: string describing result
    data: object containing relevant data (e.g., order_id, total_price, new_balance, orders list, products list)
  Examples:
    {"success": true, "message": "\u8ba2\u5355\u521b\u5efa\u6210\u529f", "data": {"order_id": 1, "total_price": 199.8}}

Functional Requirements:
[FR-001] Create Order: User creates an order with user ID and item list. Check user existence, product stock, calculate total price, deduct stock, create order record with status pending.
[FR-002] Pay Order: User pays for an order. Check order exists and status is pending, check user balance, deduct balance, update order status to paid.
[FR-003] Ship Order: Ship an order. Check order exists and status is paid, update status to shipped.
[FR-004] Complete Order: Complete an order. Check order exists and status is shipped, update status to completed.
[FR-005] Cancel Order: Cancel an order. Check order exists and status is pending or paid, restore product stock, refund balance if paid, update status to cancelled.
[FR-006] List Orders: List orders with optional filters by user and status. Display order details including items, total price, status, and time.
[FR-007] Get User Orders: Get all orders for a specific user. Calculate total spent and count of orders per status.
[FR-008] List Products: List products with info and current stock. Optionally filter low stock products.

constraints.description: Storage: memory - Three independent in-memory data sources: users, products, orders.
Concurrency: single-user, auth_required: False
UI: api
Language: Python

acceptance_criteria.description: Create order with valid user and sufficient stock succeeds, stock deducted, order created with status pending.
Create order with insufficient stock fails with error message and no stock change.
Pay order with sufficient balance succeeds, balance deducted, order status becomes paid.
Pay order with insufficient balance fails with error message.
Cancel pending order restores stock, no refund, status cancelled.
Cancel paid order restores stock and refunds balance, status cancelled.
Order status transitions correctly: pending -> paid -> shipped -> completed.
